SECTION_TITLE: Definition: IT Product
CONTENT:

CONTENT:

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Responsibility at the Organizational Structure Level
CONTENT:
• Division • Main Department • Department • Subdepartment (lowest level) In LeanIX modeling, unless explicitly stated otherwise, responsibility must always be assigned to the lowest available level of the organizational structure.
• Ensure that the assigned unit reflects the actual operational responsibility.
• This ensures clear accountability, accurate reporting, and effective governance.
subject: Responsibility

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Initiative Linkage
CONTENT:
• Initiatives must be time-bound and reflect the implementation of the Objective.
• At least one initiative must be linked to each Objective.
• This linkage ensures traceability from strategic intent to execution.
subject: Linkage

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Modeling Guidelines
CONTENT:
Additionally, it is possible—but not mandatory—to link the Digital Solution to relevant initiatives, business capabilities, and consuming organizational or processual units. These optional associations can enhance traceability and strategic alignment but are not required for the core modeling process. Summary This modeling approach ensures that Digital Solutions are only created when they are strategically relevant, aligned with value streams, and embe
subject: modeling_approach

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Characteristics of a Digital Solution
CONTENT:
• Consists of IT Products Each IT Product is an application, business service, or business platform, including its associated technologies. These are delivered to internal users (employees) and/or external users (customers).
• Supports Business Capabilities IT Products enable business capabilities, processes, and data entities.
• Enables Operational Value Streams (OVS) Digital Solutions are designed to support and enhance OVS by deliv
subject: contributing_teams

CONTENT:
• Each organizational unit may extend the existing capability map with its own capabilities as needed.
• The accuracy, maintenance, and relevance of these additions are the sole responsibility of the respective unit.
